transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling Sunrise Recovery Center, this is Sandra.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi. My name is Jacob Hodges. I'm calling about my husband. His name is Jamie. I'm... I'm not sure where to start.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Take a breath, Jacob. You're doing the right thing by calling. I can hear how hard this is. Can you tell me what's been going on?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: He's been drinking heavily for over a year. It's gotten to the point where he can't stop. He's tried a few times. Last week he had a seizure and the doctor at the ER told us he needs to go somewhere that can handle detox.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'm so sorry you and Jamie have been through that. A seizure during withdrawal is serious and I want you to know you've called the right place. We have medically supervised detox. Before I go further — is Jamie aware that you're calling today?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes. He said he's ready. He's the one who asked me to call because he was too scared to do it himself.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's really important to know. Because Jamie is an adult, anything we do in terms of treatment will require his consent — and because he's already expressed that consent to you and wants this, that makes this call something we can definitely work with. I'll need to speak with him at some point today to confirm, but let's get started with what you can share.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Of course. Whatever you need.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for understanding. Jacob, I want to acknowledge — this has clearly been frightening for you too. Spouses and families go through a lot when a loved one is struggling. How are you holding up?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Honestly not great. I've been holding everything together for a year and I'm exhausted.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's completely understandable and valid. We actually have resources for family members as well — our family counsellor can speak with you separately if that would help. We'll come back to that. Right now let's focus on getting Jamie the right help. Can you tell me about the drinking — how much, how often?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: He was drinking a fifth a day at his worst. Since the ER he's cut back but he's still drinking to avoid withdrawal.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay. So he's currently using to manage withdrawal symptoms — that's actually a sign that he needs medically supervised detox before any residential or outpatient program. Does he have any history of prior treatment?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: No. This is the first time we're looking at this.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Understood. And Jamie's insurance — is he covered under your plan or his own?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: His own, through his employer. It's CoreFirst Insurance.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Do you have his member ID and group number?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, I have his card. Member ID BP31407894, group GRP-575659.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. I have that noted. Here's what I'd like to do: I'll verify Jamie's benefits today and have someone from our admissions team call both of you — ideally with him present — within two hours. That way he can give verbal consent on the call and we can get the intake process started. Does he have a phone he can take a call on?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes. We'll both be here.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Wonderful. You'll hear from us by two o'clock this afternoon. And Jacob — what you're doing for your husband right now is an act of love. Please don't forget to take care of yourself too.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Thank you. That really helps.

gold_labels.coaching_notes: Exemplary family caller handling: consent addressed immediately, patient's autonomy respected, family member's distress acknowledged, family resources offered. Clinical screen limited by family-caller context (scored 3). Next step is precise with timing and format.
